ip_outcome_note: Card name directly names the depicted character as Son Goku in Super Saiyan 3 form. Confirmed via Bandai official listing and Union Force set roster. Frontmatter updated: ip_verified: true.

trivia_bullets.text: BT2-004 is from Union Force (DBS-B02), released November 3, 2017 — the second main booster of the DBS Card Game. The official designer note states BT2 expanded the viable deck archetype count to 20+ types across five colors. Red decks were pitched to less-experienced players built around Saiyan-family offense.
sources: dbs-cardgame.com: Union Force Red strategy page

trivia_bullets.text: Red Battle card, Rare, power 20,000 (25,000 on turn played), energy cost 4. Cannot be KO'd by opponent's skills; gains +5,000 power when played until the start of your next turn. Skill-immunity + entry-power-boost packages it as a resilient midrange threat that survives targeted removal.

Super Saiyan 3 is the third canonical Super Saiyan tier. Goku first achieved it while training in Other World (post-Cell death) and first reveals it against Majin Buu (manga chapter 472; anime episode 245). The defining visual markers — extreme hair lengthening, loss of eyebrows, massively amplified golden aura — are those the Vision section identifies on BT2-004's art. SS3 is the most physically taxing Super Saiyan form, draining ki and life span rapidly even for post-death Goku.
sources: Kanzenshuu FAQ: Super Saiyan 2 name/translation history (cross-confirms transformation numbering)
Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Super Saiyan forms

trivia_bullets.text: BT2-004 ties to the Son Goku character node as a canonical SS3-form / Buu-Saga-era print. The Son Goku corpus cluster spans five distinct arc phases across BT1 and BT2; the SS3 phase (Buu Saga) connects narratively to TB2-051 Unyielding Victory Son Goku (childhood/21st-tournament era) and the BT1 Goku prints (Battle of Gods / Saiyan Saga era).
